subject: Ecology
title: The Galápagos
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Galápagos.

hook.text: The Galápagos Islands changed biology forever when a young Darwin visited in 1835.

buildup.text: Each island's finches had different beaks—seed crushers, insect probes, cactus drillers.

discovery.text: Darwin realized these were all descendants of one mainland ancestor, shaped by local food sources.

twist.text: He didn't label which island each specimen came from—the captain's notes saved his theory.

climax.text: The Galápagos remain a living laboratory for evolution, ecology, and conservation.

punchline.text: Thirteen finches launched one of science's biggest ideas.

quiz.question: What did Darwin observe about Galápagos finches?
options: Different beaks adapted to different food sources
They could not fly between islands
